ACS (Junior) P6 Science Preliminary Examination 2025

Booklet A — Multiple Choice Questions (Q1–28, 2 marks each)

Question 1

The following are characteristics of organism X.

A   It has wings.
B   It has three body parts.
C   It has three pairs of legs.
D   It reproduces by laying eggs.

Which characteristics can be used to conclude that organism X is an insect?

  1. A and B only
  2. A and D only
  3. B and C only
  4. A, B, C and D
(3)
Question 2

Q, R, S and T represent the different stages in the life cycle of a butterfly. The butterfly is able to reproduce at stage R.

At which stage, Q, R, S and T, is the butterfly considered a pest to farmers?

  1. Q
  2. R
  3. S
  4. T
(3)
Question 3

Scientists observed that Island P had no plants or animals. A few years later, they found new plants growing there. The diagram shows Island P and a nearby Island Y.

They also observed that some fruits that were found on Island Y were also present on Island P.

Which of the fruits A, B, C and/or D are most likely to be found on both islands?

  1. C only
  2. D only
  3. A, B and C only
  4. A, B, C and D
(4)
Question 4

Process X takes place in the human female reproductive system.

In which part of the flower Z does a similar process occur?

  1. A
  2. B
  3. C
  4. D
(3)
Question 5

The table shows the main functions of organs W, X, Y and Z that are part of the human digestive system.

FunctionOrgan(s)
Digestion of foodW, X, Z
Absorption of digested foodW
Absorption of waterY

What are organs W, X, Y and Z?

WXYZ
(1)StomachMouthLarge intestineSmall intestine
(2)StomachLarge intestineSmall intestineMouth
(3)Small intestineStomachLarge intestineMouth
(4)Small intestineMouthStomachLarge intestine
(3)
Question 6

Three of the leaves from the pot of plant were wrapped with three different types of plastic bags of the same size as shown in the table. The pot of plant was left under bright sunlight for some time.

LeafABC
Type of plastic bagClear plastic bagClear plastic bag with holesBlack plastic bag

Which of the following graphs correctly represents the amount of food made by leaves, A, B and C, after five hours?

(3)
Question 7

The diagram shows the different paths taken by food and water in a plant with parts A, B, C and D.

Which of the following represents A, B, C and D?

ABCD
(1)rootstemfruitfruit
(2)rootstemfruitleaf
(3)flowerleafrootstem
(4)stemrootleafflower
(1)
Question 8

A group of students carried out an investigation to test their hypothesis.

They did the following:

  • Rest for 2 minutes and record initial breathing rate.
  • Perform different exercises (walk, jump and run) for 1 minute each.
  • Count their breathing rate for 30 seconds immediately after each exercise.
  • Record all data in a table.
  • Create a bar graph to compare results.

Which hypothesis is the most suitable for this investigation?

  1. The higher the exercise intensity, the higher the breathing rate.
  2. The lower the oxygen level in the air, the higher the breathing rate.
  3. The higher the surrounding air temperature, the higher the breathing rate.
  4. The longer the rest period between exercises, the lower the breathing rate.
(1)
Question 9

Study the flowchart.

Where are cells, P, Q and R, taken from?

PQR
(1)Leaf of plantRoot of plantSkin of human
(2)Leaf of plantFlower of plantStem of plant
(3)Stem of plantSkin of humanRoot of plant
(4)Stem of plantRoot of plantRoot of plant
(1)
Question 10

Study the food web.

Which one of the following shows the correct relationship among the various organisms in the food web?

Prey onlyPredator onlyBoth prey and predator
(1)CEA, B, D and F
(2)CA and EB, D and F
(3)D and FA and EB
(4)D and FAB
(3)
Question 11

The graph shows the change in the area covered by plant G in a garden. Animal X was introduced to the garden community at week 4.

Which of the statements could explain the increase in the area covered by plant G after week 4?

A   Animal X helped plant G disperse its seeds.
B   Animal X preyed on animals that fed on plant G.
C   Animal X fed on plant G and plant G reproduced.
D   The waste from animal X helped plant G grow faster.

  1. A and D only
  2. B and C only
  3. A, B and D only
  4. B, C and D only
(3)
Question 12

Three organisms, R, S and T, have characteristics that help them survive in their habitat as shown in the table.

OrganismCharacteristic
RHave needle-shaped leaves
SHave a thick, waxy cuticle
TTrap air spaces in leaf stalk

Which of the following organisms' characteristics have been correctly matched to their function?

To breathe in waterTo reduce water lossTo float on water
(1)TSR
(2)TRS
(3)SRT
(4)STR
(2)
Question 13

The PSI (Pollutant Standards Index) gives an indication of air quality. The table shows what the air quality is like based on the PSI value.

PSI ValueAir Quality
1 – 50Good
51 – 100Moderate
101 – 200Unhealthy
201 – 300Very Unhealthy
Above 300Hazardous

The PSI value of a town over five days are shown in the table.

Day12345
PSI values4648123147144

Which one of the following is most likely to have caused the PSI value to increase from Day 2 to Day 4?

  1. A few trees were cut down.
  2. More forest fires happened nearby.
  3. More farmers started growing vegetables.
  4. More people switched from cars to electrical cars.
(2)
Question 14

Plants use water and gas to make food through the process of photosynthesis.

Which of the following best represents the letters A, B, C, D and E during photosynthesis?

ABCDE
(1)HeatCarbon DioxideOxygenGlucoseChlorophyll
(2)SunlightOxygenCarbon DioxideGlucoseChlorophyll
(3)HeatOxygenOxygenWaterGlucose
(4)SunlightOxygenCarbon DioxideWaterGlucose
(4)
Question 15

Mr Chan used two set-ups, A and B, to conduct an investigation. When he poured water into set-up A, only a small amount of water flowed into the flask as shown. However, when he poured the same amount of water into set-up B, all the water flowed in.

Which of the following best explains Mr Chan's observation?

  1. Air in set-up B was compressed.
  2. Water in set-up B was compressed.
  3. The glass tube in set-up B allowed air to escape.
  4. The water in set-up A occupied the space in the flask so more water could not flow in.
(3)

The diagram shows the water cycle.

Which of the following correctly identifies X, Y and Z?

XYZ
(1)condensationevaporationsnow
(2)condensationcondensationcloud
(3)evaporationcondensationsnow
(4)evaporationcondensationcloud
(4)
Question 17

Four identical containers, P, Q, R and S, were filled with the same amount of water. They were placed at four different locations under varying conditions for a day as shown.

The conditions remained constant throughout the experiment.

Which of the following graphs shows the possible amount of water left in containers P, Q, R and S after one day?

(1)
Question 18

The diagram shows four circuits with identical batteries and bulbs.

Which of the bulbs, A, B, C or D, will be the least bright?

  1. A
  2. B
  3. C
  4. D
(1)
Question 19

The diagram shows a toy with an electric circuit.

The table shows the observations when the two switches in the circuit are open and/or closed.

Switch 1Switch 2Observation
ClosedOpenLight only
OpenClosedMusic only
ClosedClosedMusic and light
OpenOpenNo music and light

Which of the following represents the circuit in the toy?

(1)
Question 20

The diagram shows three magnets with ends, CD, EF and GH.

Which ends will repel?

  1. C and F
  2. C and H
  3. D and G
  4. D and H
(4)
Question 21

Ali strokes a nail 50 times with a magnet as shown.

He brought the nail near a pile of iron pins and observed that it did not attract any of the iron pins.

Which of the following explains his observation?

  1. He stroked the nail in the wrong direction.
  2. The nail had become a temporary magnet.
  3. The nail is made of non-magnetic material.
  4. He used the North pole of the magnet to stroke the nail.
(3)
Question 22

Marble R was rolling from point X on a carpeted floor. It was hit by marble S causing a change. Marble R continued moving and eventually stopped at point Y as shown.

Based on the experiment, what can be concluded about the effects of forces acting on marble R?

A   A force can stop a moving object.
B   A force can change the size of an object.
C   A force can change the direction of a moving object.

  1. A and B only
  2. A and C only
  3. B and C only
  4. A, B and C
(2)
Question 23

Belle hung two identical objects, A and B, on two identical springs. Object A was hung in the air while object B was hung in a container of sand as shown. She then lifted the two springs with the object upwards by 5cm.

Which one of the following statements is incorrect?

  1. More force is needed to lift object B.
  2. Gravitational force is acting on objects A and B.
  3. More gravitational force is acting on object A than on object B.
  4. There is more force acting downwards on object B due to the sand.
(3)
Question 24

Sea turtle hatchlings, the young of turtles, crawl to the sea after hatching. They are guided by light reflected off the water as they make their way to the sea. However, bright lights from nearby carparks can confuse them.

Which streetlamp, A, B, C or D, is least likely to confuse hatchlings?

  1. A
  2. B
  3. C
  4. D
(2)
Question 25

Dave attached three identical pieces of wax, A, B and C, to a metal frame with equal sides as shown. When Dave heated the frame at a particular spot, the wax melted in the following order, B, A, C.

At which spot, W, X, Y or Z, did Dave heat the metal frame?

  1. W
  2. X
  3. Y
  4. Z
(1)
Question 26

Maverick set up an experiment as shown to compare how well four rods, A, B, C and D, made of different materials, conduct heat. The similar-sized rods at 30°C were placed in a container of crushed ice at the same time.

The table shows the temperature of each rod after five minutes.

RodTemperature (°C)
A20
B14
C8
D25

What can Maverick conclude about the heat conductivity of the materials of the rods, A, B, C and D?

  1. D is the best conductor of heat.
  2. C is the poorest conductor of heat.
  3. B is a better conductor of heat than C.
  4. C is a better conductor of heat than D.
(3)
Question 27

The diagram shows a metal spoon in a bowl of ice cream.

Which graph shows the changes in temperature of the metal spoon and the ice cream over time?

  1. Graph (1)
  2. Graph (2)
  3. Graph (3)
  4. Graph (4)
(2)
Question 28

Jace carried out an experiment with a pendulum. He lifted the bob to position A and let it swing to position B, C and then back to X.

Which graph shows how the gravitational potential energy of the bob changes as it swings from position A to C and to X?

  1. Graph (1)
  2. Graph (2)
  3. Graph (3)
  4. Graph (4)
(4)

Booklet B — Open-Ended Questions (Q29–40)

Question 29

Jinu measured the heart rate of five children while they were listening to slow-paced music, no music and fast-paced music.

He recorded his results in the table.

ChildHeart Rate (per minute)
Slow-paced MusicNo MusicFast-paced Music
A7885110
B738398
C687488
D727990
E708092
(a)

State the aim of Jinu's experiment. [1]

His aim of the experiment is to find out how the pace of the music affects the heart rate (per minute).
(b)

State the relationship between the pace of music that children listen to and their heart rate. [1]

As the pace of the music that children listen to increases, their heart rate increases.
(c)

Suggest one improvement to Jinu's experiment to obtain more accurate results. [1]

He can repeat the experiment at least 3 times and take the average results to ensure consistency.
Question 30

The diagram shows a pond community.

(a)

How many populations are shown in the diagram? [1]

4
(b)

The graph shows the amount of oxygen in the pond from the time when muddy water from a nearby farm was washed into the pond.

The amount of oxygen in the pond decreased over time. Explain why. [1]

As the muddy water is washed into the pond, the dirt from the mud blocks light from the Sun from reaching plant H. With less light trapped by H, the rate of photosynthesis decreases and less oxygen is produced over time.
(c)

Which organism shown in the diagram is most likely to survive? Explain why. [1]

Frog. Frog is an amphibian and it can take in oxygen through its skin/lungs when there is less oxygen in the water.
Question 31

Monkey A has a short tail and thick fur that is light-brown in colour. Monkey B has a long tail and thick fur that is red in colour.

(a)

A dry open grassland has yellow coloured grass and very few trees while a rainforest has many tall trees.

Which monkey survives better in each habitat? Explain why. [2]

Grassland – Monkey A has light-brown coloured thick fur that can camouflage and blend in with the yellow coloured grass, so it is less likely to be spotted by predators. Rainforest – Monkey B has a long tail that allows it to hang and swing from tall tree to tall tree to escape from predators more quickly.
(b)

At night, the surrounding temperature of both the grassland and rainforest decreases by at least 5°C.

Explain how having thick fur is advantageous to both monkeys. [1]

The thick fur allows them to trap a layer of air. Air is a poor conductor of heat and reduces the heat lost from the body, thus keeping them warm.
Question 32

James observed flower C and recorded the following observations.

  • It has no scent.
  • Its petals are small and dull in colour.
  • Its anthers and feathery stigma hang outside the petals.
(a)

State how flower C is likely pollinated. [1]

Wind
(b)

James also observed two other flowers, D and E, as shown.

(i)

Which flower, D or E, is able to develop into a fruit? Explain why. [1]

Flower D. It has stigma to receive pollen grains from the bees during pollination. Flower E does not have both stigma and ovary.
(ii)

James noticed bees visiting both flowers. State how the bees and the flowers benefit from each other. [2]

The bees help to transfer pollen grains from the anthers of flower E to the stigma of flower D, thus pollination can take place (followed by fertilisation). The bees get nectar as a source of food from the flowers.
Question 33

Minghe bought hot and iced coffee from a cafe. The coffee was served in paper cups with clear plastic lids. He noticed that there was a plastic coating inside the cups.

(a)

Minghe's left hand felt warm when he was holding the cup of hot coffee and his right hand felt cold when holding the cup of iced coffee. Explain why. [1]

His left hand gained heat from the hot coffee, making his hand feel warm. His right hand lost heat to the iced coffee, making his hand feel cold.
(b)

Minghe could see through the clear plastic lids but not through the paper cup. Explain why. [1]

The clear plastic lid is transparent and allows light to pass through and lands on the coffee. The paper cup then reflects the light into his eyes. The paper cup is opaque and does not allow any light to pass through, hence he is unable to see through.
(c)

Minghe observed that the paper cups containing cold coffee did not leak. He thinks the plastic coating inside the cups prevents leakage. Suggest a way he can test his idea. [1]

He can give an equal amount of water at the same temperature into two cups. One cup has the plastic coating inside and the other cup does not have any plastic coating. He should observe if there is any water leaking out from the cup without any plastic coating.
Question 34

A security guard needs to control two 'slow down' signs at different entrance gates. Each entrance gate has its own bulb that lights up the sign. The guard must be able to switch on or off the bulb for each sign independently, depending on the traffic situation at each gate.

(a)

The diagram shows part of the circuit. Complete the circuit so that it will work as described. [3]

Circuit diagram with two switches (one per gate) in parallel, each controlling its own bulb, connected to a battery.
(b)

State an advantage of the way you have connected the bulbs in part (a). [1]

When one of the bulbs fuses, the other bulb remains lit when it is switched on.
Question 35

Jarrell wanted to investigate which glass, A or B, keeps water cold for a longer time. Both glasses were made of the same material.

He measured the temperature of water in each glass at the start of the experiment and every 10 minutes for 50 minutes. He plotted the results in the graph.

(a)

Complete the table by matching the lines X and Y to glasses, A and B. [1]

LineGlass
X
Y
X: B, Y: A
(b)

State two variables that Jarrell must keep constant to conduct a fair test. [1]

Any two – The amount of water used at the start of the experiment; the starting temperature of the water; the type/size of glasses.
(c)

State the increase in the temperature of the water in glass A when it reached room temperature. [1]

20°C
(d)

The table shows the daily average temperature for Singapore during the day.

Average temperature (°C) in the day
Inside the house28°C
Outside the house32°C

Are double-layered or single-layered glass windows more suitable for houses in Singapore, so that it is cooler inside the house? Explain your answer. [2]

Double-layered. The temperature of water increases less for glass A when compared to B over 50 minutes. The layer of air trapped between the glass layers is a poor conductor of heat. This reduces the heat gained from the warmer surrounding air outside the house to the inside of the house, keeping the inside cooler.
Question 36

Elliott visits a new eco-friendly playground where children's movements and/or energy from solar panels are used to generate electricity and light up the playground. The wheel is connected to a generator which allows electricity to be produced to power the lights in the playground.

(a)

Where does Elliott's energy come from? [1]

Food he ate.
(b)

Fill in the boxes to show the conversion of energy when a child is running on the wheel. [1]

energy (wheel turning) → energy (generator) → energy (LED lights)

Kinetic Energy → Electrical Energy → Light Energy
(c)

State one advantage of the solar panels. [1]

Any one – It uses a renewable source of energy. / It does not produce pollution. / It does not produce harmful gases.
Question 37

John set up a circuit that is fixed to a box with a seesaw attached to the top where the ducks can move up and/or down. The seesaw has identical plastic ducks with iron rings of different masses attached as shown.

(a)

When John closed the switch, both ducks moved up and/or down continuously. Explain why. [2]

When the switch is closed, electric current flows through the circuit and the iron bar is magnetised to become an electromagnet. The electromagnet attracts the iron ring under duck A, pulling duck A down. When duck A is pulled down, the metal contacts separate and the circuit is broken. The electromagnet is demagnetised. As the ring under B has more mass, more gravitational force acts on it, pulling duck B down. The metal contacts touch again and the cycle repeats.
(b)

What could John do to the circuit to make the ducks move up and/or down faster? [1]

Any one – Increase number of batteries / Increase number of coils around iron bar.
(c)

John replaced the 5g iron ring with another 5g metal ring. He made sure that the metal contacts touched the 10g iron ring. When he closed the switch, he observed that duck B moved up but it did not move back down. Explain why. [2]

The new metal ring is a magnet. When the electromagnet attracts the iron ring under duck B and duck B moves up, the circuit is broken. However, the permanent magnet under duck A continues to attract the iron ring of duck B, keeping duck B up and preventing it from moving back down.
Question 38

Meiling conducted an experiment by placing a 1 kg block on a flat surface and measured the amount of force needed to pull it along the surface using a spring balance.

She repeated the experiment using three similar blocks that are of different masses and recorded the results in the graph.

(a)

State what Meiling could conclude based on the results of her experiment. [1]

As the mass of the block increases, the amount of force needed to pull it across the floor increases.
(b)

Meiling read in the news that the MRT trains kept breaking down frequently due to wear and tear of the train wheels. She also read that there was an increase in the number of passengers over a period of time.

Explain how an increase in the number of passengers could cause frequent train breakdowns. [2]

When the number of passengers on the train increases, the mass of the train with passengers increases. That results in a greater amount of frictional force between the metal wheels and metal train tracks. There is greater wear and tear on the wheels.
Question 39

Siti packed some clothes into a bag and sealed it.

(a)

Identify the state(s) of matter in the sealed bag. [1]

Solid and Gas
(b)

Siti wants to find out the mass of air that can be removed from the bag by pressing down on the bag.

(i)

Using an electronic balance, describe the steps she needs to take to find the mass of air that can be removed. [2]

Step 1: Measure and record the mass of the bag with clothes using an electronic balance. Step 2: Press down on the bag to remove as much air from the bag as possible. Step 3: Measure and record the mass of the bag with clothes with air removed. Step 4: Find the difference between the two measurements to find out the mass of air that can be removed.
(ii)

Will the volume of the bag with clothes increase, decrease or remain the same after air is removed? Explain your answer using the physical property of air. [1]

Decrease. Air is a matter and it occupies space. With less air, it occupies less space in the bag.
Question 40
(a)

State what evaporation is. [1]

Evaporation is a process where a liquid gains heat from a heat source to become gas at any temperature.

After washing his hands, Mr Tan placed his wet hands, in a clasped manner, under a wall-mounted hand dryer as shown. The hand dryer gives out hot air at a high speed.

Mr Tan noticed that his hands dried faster when he used the hand dryer.

(b)(i)

State two reasons why the hand dryer helps to dry his hands in a shorter time. [1]

The hot air provides more heat and more wind provided by the hot air moving at high speed.
(b)(ii)

Explain your answer in part (b)(i). [1]

The water on his hands gained heat from the hot air and evaporated faster. The hot air moving at high speed provides wind to the water on his hands, causing the water to evaporate faster.
(b)(iii)

Mr Tan spread out his hands instead of clasping them under the hand dryer. Will his hands take a longer or shorter time to dry? Explain your answer. [1]

Shorter. When his hands are spread out, it increases the exposed surface area of the water on his hands and increases the rate of evaporation of water, causing more water on his hands to evaporate in a shorter time.
